title: Somalia's Federal Parliament Mandate Expires — Speaker Declares Unilateral One-Year Extension; Farmaajo Warns of Leadership Vacuum
detail: Somalia's 11th Federal Parliament formally expired its four-year constitutional mandate on April 14, 2026, with no elections held or agreed upon. Parliament Speaker Adan Madobe unilaterally announced a one-year extension of parliament's term, citing provisions under the new (contested) constitution to facilitate transition to a 'one-person, one-vote' electoral system. The announcement was immediately condemned by opposition lawmakers, Puntland, and Jubbaland as unconstitutional and illegitimate. Former President Mohamed Abdullahi 'Farmaajo' issued a statement warning Somalia was entering a 'leadership vacuum,' with multiple former prime ministers echoing warnings of a 'constitutional vacuum.' Several lawmakers reportedly contemplated resignation in protest. The Federal Government briefed foreign ambassadors in Mogadishu about the one-year extension rationale, but international partners — including the UN, AU, and EU — remained silent on endorsement. The mandate expiry was the climax of the constitutional crisis triggered by the FGS's contested March 2026 constitutional amendments, which extended parliamentary terms from four to five years in a disputed vote lacking the required quorum. Constitutional scholars from the ConstitutionNet noted that Article 60 and Article 91 of Somalia's Provisional Constitution provide no clear authority for a unilateral parliamentary self-extension beyond the four-year term. With the presidential mandate also due to expire May 15, 2026, Somalia faces cascading constitutional deadlines without an agreed electoral road map.

media.caption: Somalia's Federal Parliament mandate expires April 14 with no elections; Speaker announces unilateral one-year extension

title: AUSSOM Sector 1 Troops Distribute Food and Medicine to Mubarak Residents
detail: AUSSOM (AU Mission in Somalia) Sector 1 troops — led by the Uganda People's Defence Force — conducted a humanitarian assistance operation in Mubarak, Lower Shabelle on April 14, 2026, distributing food and medicine to local residents. Mubarak was recaptured from Al-Shabaab during Operation Rolling Thunder / Hilaac (February–March 2026) and has since become a critical forward position for consolidating gains in the Lower Shabelle corridor. The humanitarian outreach is part of a deliberate AUSSOM strategy to rebuild civilian confidence in liberated areas and reduce Al-Shabaab's ability to re-infiltrate through community grievance. SNA and AUSSOM troops repelled a major Al-Shabaab assault on Mubarak on March 26, killing 40 militants, underscoring the town's strategic importance. AUSSOM Sector 1 has also been conducting healthcare training for Somali medical personnel as part of capacity-building efforts in stabilized areas.

media.caption: AUSSOM Sector 1 (UPDF) troops distribute humanitarian aid to residents of Mubarak, Lower Shabelle, recaptured from Al-Shabaab in March 2026
